reforms.description: Costa Rica is developing a framework for the assessment of the quality of education, which will provide guidance on teacher evaluation and feedback mechanisms. This initiative aims to enhance the quality of teaching and learning outcomes in the education system.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving teacher quality is expected to enhance educational outcomes, which can lead to a more skilled workforce and increased productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: Costa Rica is developing a framework for the assessment of the quality of education.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to systematically improve teacher assessment and feedback, which is crucial for enhancing educational quality.
reforms.source_quote: Costa Rica is developing a framework for the assessment of the quality of education that has to be presented for approval to the Higher Council of Education.

reforms.description: Costa Rica is reforming the funding mechanism for public universities to create incentives for better alignment with national educational goals, including enhancing research quality and supporting equitable access to education.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By linking funding to performance and national goals, this reform is expected to improve the quality and relevance of higher education, fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Modifying the design of the funding system of public universities could steer the allocation of funds to encourage them to pursue national goals.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in how public universities are funded, aiming to enhance accountability and align educational outcomes with national priorities.
reforms.source_quote: Modifying the design of the funding system of public universities could steer the allocation of funds to encourage them to pursue national goals.

reforms.description: Costa Rica is revisiting university funding mechanisms to improve accountability and responsiveness to labor market needs, particularly in STEM fields.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing university funding mechanisms is expected to improve the quality of education and align it with labor market demands, thereby increasing employability and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is revisiting university funding mechanisms to improve accountability and responsiveness.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a critical issue in education funding, aiming to enhance the quality of STEM education and better align it with labor market needs, which is significant but not a complete overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: Revisiting universities funding mechanisms can improve accountability and the responsiveness to labour market needs.

reforms.description: Costa Rica is strengthening vocational education reforms to increase the supply and quality of technicians, addressing skills mismatches in the labor market.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving vocational education, the reform aims to reduce skills mismatches and enhance the employability of graduates, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Recent reforms in vocational education aim at increasing the supply and quality of technicians.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it targets a specific area of education that is crucial for addressing labor market needs, but it does not represent a complete overhaul of the education system.
reforms.source_quote: Recent reforms in vocational education aim at increasing the supply and quality of technicians.

reforms.description: Costa Rica is working towards improving the digital signature mechanism to facilitate compliance with administrative procedures, which is expected to enhance the ease of doing business and support the formalization of firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the digital signature mechanism is likely to reduce bureaucratic hurdles, thereby encouraging formalization and improving business efficiency.
reforms.status_evidence: The Central Bank is currently working towards a solution that would enable the use of the digital signature via mobile phones.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a significant barrier to business operations and formalization, which can have a positive impact on the overall business environment.
reforms.source_quote: The Central Bank is currently working towards a solution that would enable the use of the digital signature via mobile phones.

reforms.description: Costa Rica has published a law to promote regulatory consistency throughout the country, aimed at facilitating the deployment of telecommunications infrastructure.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving regulatory consistency is expected to lower entry barriers for new operators, enhancing competition and access to telecommunications services.
reforms.status_evidence: The law has been published to promote regulatory consistency.
reforms.importance_rationale: The law represents a significant step towards improving the regulatory environment for telecommunications, which is crucial for digital transformation.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities have recently published a law to promote regulatory consistency throughout the country, to promote and facilitate the deployment of infrastructure.

reforms.description: A law has been approved to enable the use of public spaces for installing telecommunications infrastructure, ensuring that technical aspects are considered in national road planning.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This law is expected to enhance the deployment of telecommunications infrastructure, which is vital for improving connectivity and supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The law has been approved to enable the use of public spaces for telecommunications infrastructure.
reforms.importance_rationale: The approval of this law is a significant measure to facilitate infrastructure deployment, which is essential for enhancing digital services.
reforms.source_quote: A law has also been approved to enable the use of public spaces to install telecommunications infrastructure.

reforms.description: Costa Rica aims to implement the National Programme of Digital Literacy to ensure access to digital technology and skills across the country, addressing educational inequality due to technological vulnerability.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing digital skills is essential for employability and integration into modern society, which can boost productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme is still pending implementation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative addresses a critical gap in digital skills, which is vital for future economic competitiveness and social inclusion.
reforms.source_quote: The National Programme of Digital Literacy aims to ensure access to connectivity throughout the country within five years.

reforms.description: Costa Rica is expanding government-backed student loans with repayment terms based on future income to promote enrolment in fields of national interest, particularly in STEM areas. This initiative aims to increase access to higher education for students from vulnerable backgrounds.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing access to higher education, particularly in STEM fields, the reform aims to enhance the skill set of the workforce, which can lead to higher productivity and innovation in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The use of government-backed student loans could be expanded for students with a middle-class background.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a significant barrier to higher education access for vulnerable groups, which is crucial for improving human capital and meeting labor market demands.
reforms.source_quote: The use of government-backed student loans, with repayment terms based on future income, could be expanded for students with a middle-class background.

reforms.description: Costa Rica aims to streamline regulation in public and private universities to facilitate the creation of new courses and programmes, particularly in hybrid or distance modes. This reform is intended to enhance the responsiveness of higher education to labor market needs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By making it easier to introduce new educational programs, the reform is expected to better align educational offerings with labor market demands, thus enhancing employability and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: Streamlining regulation in public and private universities would facilitate the opening of new courses and programmes.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses regulatory barriers that hinder the responsiveness of higher education institutions to labor market needs, which is critical for improving the quality and relevance of education.
reforms.source_quote: Regulation is a major obstacle to the creation of new courses or degrees.

reforms.description: The 2021 reform of the National Vocational Training Institute (INA) broadens its goals to include providing technical support for firm development, especially for SMEs, and introduces a new system of grants and economic-aids aimed at increasing the number of beneficiaries from vulnerable groups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the employability of graduates and supporting SMEs, the reform aims to improve the overall skill level in the workforce, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been enacted and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the goals and operations of INA, aiming to better align vocational training with labor market needs and improve the employability of graduates, which is crucial for economic development.
reforms.source_quote: The reform sets as INA’s explicit goals increasing the employability of its graduates, promoting entrepreneurship and providing technical and financial support for firms’ development, especially SMEs.

reforms.description: Costa Rica aims to streamline regulation to facilitate the opening of new programmes in tertiary education, reducing the uncertainty faced by private universities in the authorization process.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By simplifying regulations, the reform is expected to enhance the responsiveness of educational institutions to market needs, thereby fostering innovation and improving educational outcomes.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform aims to reduce the uncertainty of the authorization process faced by private universities.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in the regulatory framework for tertiary education, which could lead to increased innovation in educational offerings and better alignment with labor market demands.
reforms.source_quote: Streamline regulation to facilitate the opening of new programmes and reduce the uncertainty of the authorization process faced by private universities.
